description:
Today's codegen convention: prims default to producing Tagged FzValue and consumers unbox per their needs (`as_raw_i64` etc.). This was right when raw kinds didn't exist; after the .27 arc it's backwards. The typer knows each var's Descr; the prim should produce the form that matches.

## Motivation — add1.fz still has gratuitous box/unbox

Even after .27.14 (cont-boundary cleanup), main and add1's CLIF show:

```
v2 = iconst.i64 329          ; tagged 41 (= 41<<3|1)
v3 = sshr_imm v2, 3          ; unbox to 41
v4 = call fn0(v3, v1)        ; add1_s2 wants raw — was raw, then tagged, now raw again
```

and inside add1_s2:

```
v3 = iconst.i64 9            ; tagged 1
v4 = sshr_imm v3, 3          ; unbox to 1
v5 = iadd v0, v4
```

`Const::Int(n)` at `src/ir_codegen.rs:3624` unconditionally emits `iconst((n<<3)|TAG_INT)`. Every int-arithmetic and RawInt-slot consumer then unboxes via `as_raw_i64`. The same shape applies to `Const::Float(f)` (line 3629) which `fz_alloc_float`s every literal — every float consumer immediately `unbox_float`s.

For add1.fz under raw-by-default, the floor is **zero box/unbox ops anywhere** (since `print/1` already dispatches to `fz_print_i64(raw)` when the Descr is int — line 3905).

## Why this is just plumbing now

- `tagged_get` (line 4292) already exists and already handles `raw_int_vars` / `raw_f64_vars` via `box_int` / `box_float_native`.
- Every stmt-level Tagged-expecting consumer (BinOp slow path, all `Prim::Builtin` arms, `ListCons`, `TupleField`, `MakeStruct`, etc.) already routes through `tagged_get`.
- Direct `env.get` access is confined to `tagged_get` / `as_raw_i64` / `as_raw_f64` themselves (verified by grep).

So flipping a const producer from Tagged to Raw is sound today; the consumer machinery is already symmetric. The Tagged producers stayed Tagged purely by historical inertia.

## DAG

- **.27.15.1** Const::Int emits RawI64 directly when the var's Descr is int-monomorphic. The literal `iconst N` (raw) replaces `iconst (N<<3)|TAG_INT`. Tagged consumers retag via `tagged_get` / `box_int` — same op count for them, zero ops for raw consumers.
- **.27.15.2** Const::Float emits RawF64 directly via `f64const(f)`. Eliminates the per-literal `fz_alloc_float` heap call when the const is consumed raw. Tagged consumers heap-alloc on demand via `box_float_native` (same op count for them).

## Out of scope (follow-up if/when motivated)

- Generalize to other bucket-3 prims (TupleField, ListHead/Tail, BinOp slow-path results, etc.) that *could* return raw when their slot/Descr matches. Each is its own audit; not blocked by .15.1/.15.2 and probably worth separate tickets per-prim-family.

## Acceptance (epic)

- `cargo test` green, no warnings.
- add1.fz CLIF: zero `ishl_imm`/`bor_imm`/`sshr_imm` ops in `main`, `add1_s2`, or `k_2_s3` (boxing of the Nil-bits literal is not boxing — it's the value's tag).
- No regression in spec-parity, fixture matrix, or existing CLIF goldens.
title: [EPIC] Raw-by-default for monomorphic int/float prims
